front: How does Flink handle late data — what are allowed lateness and side outputs?
back: When using event-time windows, events arriving after the window's watermark has passed are 'late.' By default, Flink drops late data. Allowed lateness lets you specify an additional wait period after the watermark: `.allowedLateness(Time.minutes(2))` keeps the window state open for 2 extra minutes and re-fires the window each time a late element arrives. This is useful when occasional late data should update results without full re-computation. For data that arrives even later than the allowed lateness, side outputs capture those events into a separate stream for auditing or DLQ processing: define an OutputTag and call `.sideOutputLateData(lateTag)` on the window; then retrieve with `mainStream.getSideOutput(lateTag)`. The tradeoff: larger allowed lateness = more state held in memory, longer before results are final, and more re-emissions. Set based on your SLA and observed data delay patterns.
